task: I want to make one genuinely good winter purchase instead of impulse-buying the first heated blanket I see. Please do a serious browser comparison of 12 current electric heated blanket options across major public retailer pages and manufacturer pages where available, focusing on products that are realistically usable for bed warmth in winter in the U.S. Include a mix of at least 4 full-size bed blankets, at least 4 throws, and at least 4 larger or premium options for shared use or colder sleepers. For each option, record the product name, retailer, current listed price, size, material, number of heat settings, auto shutoff details, machine-washability, controller style, safety certification or compliance language if shown, warranty length if shown, and shipping or delivery info if it is prominently visible; use 'not shown' when a field is missing. Open the actual listing pages for all candidates, and for at least 6 of them also open either the manufacturer product page, care/manual page, or another public page that helps verify the safety or care details, so the comparison is not based on retailer copy alone. While comparing, look through photos and review sections closely enough to capture recurring positives and negatives for each product, especially anything about uneven heating, reliability, shedding, thinness, controller annoyance, or washing problems. Then narrow the 12 down to a final shortlist of 5 with a clear recommendation for best overall, best value, best throw, best for couples or larger beds, and best premium pick. End with one concise buying memo that explains which single blanket I should buy and why, and keep the 5 finalist product tabs plus the 2 most useful verification tabs open so I can review them.
grader_reasoning:
R1: failure - The agent failed to perform the required comparison. It only performed initial searches and did not compile the list of 12 candidates, nor did it categorize them into the required 4/4/4 mix.

R2: failure - The agent did not produce a comparison table or any record containing the required fields (price, size, material, etc.) for 12 products.

R3: failure - The agent did not open the required 12 retailer listing pages or the 6 verification pages.

R4: failure - The agent did not capture review-based pros/cons for the candidates.

R5: failure - The agent did not narrow down to a shortlist of 5 or provide the required recommendations.

R6: failure - The agent did not provide a final buying memo.

R7: failure - The agent did not leave the required 7 tabs open at the end of the session.

rubric_results.requirement: Exactly 12 current heated blanket candidates are compared from public pages, including at least 4 full-size bed blankets, at least 4 throws, and at least 4 larger or premium options for shared use or colder sleepers.
rubric_results.verification: The final comparison includes 12 distinct products and clearly identifies their type/category so the required 4/4/4 coverage can be checked from the recorded entries and open product tabs.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent failed to perform the required comparison. It only performed initial searches and did not compile the list of 12 candidates, nor did it categorize them into the required 4/4/4 mix.

rubric_results.requirement: For each of the 12 options, the comparison records the product name, retailer, current listed price, size, material, number of heat settings, auto shutoff details, machine-washability, controller style, safety certification or compliance language if shown, warranty length if shown, and shipping or delivery info if prominently visible, using 'not shown' when needed.
rubric_results.verification: Each of the 12 products has all requested fields filled in or explicitly marked 'not shown' in the final comparison.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not produce a comparison table or any record containing the required fields (price, size, material, etc.) for 12 products.

rubric_results.requirement: All 12 candidates have their actual retailer listing pages opened, and at least 6 of them also have a manufacturer product page, care/manual page, or other public verification page opened to confirm safety or care details.
rubric_results.verification: Open tabs show the 12 retailer listings and at least 6 additional verification pages tied to specific candidates.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not open the required 12 retailer listing pages or the 6 verification pages.

rubric_results.requirement: The comparison captures recurring review and photo-based positives and negatives for each product, with attention to uneven heating, reliability, shedding, thinness, controller annoyance, and washing problems when those themes appear.
rubric_results.verification: Each candidate includes brief synthesized pros/cons or review-theme notes drawn from its public listing and supporting pages.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not capture review-based pros/cons for the candidates.

rubric_results.requirement: The 12 products are narrowed to a final shortlist of exactly 5, and the shortlist includes a named recommendation for best overall, best value, best throw, best for couples or larger beds, and best premium pick.
rubric_results.verification: The final output presents exactly 5 finalists and assigns each of the five requested recommendation labels.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not narrow down to a shortlist of 5 or provide the required recommendations.

rubric_results.requirement: A concise final buying memo recommends one single blanket to buy and explains why it wins over the other finalists.
rubric_results.verification: The final memo explicitly names one product as the buy recommendation and gives a comparative rationale grounded in the recorded features, verification details, and review themes.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not provide a final buying memo.

rubric_results.requirement: The 5 finalist product tabs and the 2 most useful verification tabs are left open at the end for review.
rubric_results.verification: The final browser state shows 7 kept-open tabs: 5 finalist product pages and 2 verification pages.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent did not leave the required 7 tabs open at the end of the session.
